title: FAA documents outline SpaceX plans for Starfall reentry vehicles
summary: FAA documents reveal SpaceX's plans to develop and test Starfall reentry vehicles, which could support in-space manufacturing projects. The project aims to enhance SpaceX's capabilities in returning materials from space.

title: Blue Origin gets national security launch task order hours before New Glenn explosion
summary: Blue Origin received a national security launch task order from the U.S. Space Force and National Reconnaissance Office just hours before the explosion of its New Glenn rocket. The agencies reaffirmed their commitment to Blue Origin as a partner despite the incident.

title: Space Force awards SpaceX $4.16 billion to build satellite network for airborne target tracking
summary: The U.S. Space Force has awarded SpaceX a $4.16 billion contract to develop a low-orbit satellite constellation for tracking airborne threats such as aircraft and cruise missiles. This initiative aims to enhance the military's surveillance and defense capabilities.

title: Space Force’s commercial gatekeeper offers a playbook for startups seeking defense business
summary: The U.S. Space Force's commercial gatekeeper, Col. Tim Trimailo, advises startups to prioritize transparency, patience, and a clear military value proposition alongside technical innovation when seeking defense contracts. This guidance aims to help startups navigate the defense business landscape effectively.

title: Bellatrix and TelePIX plan 2028 air-breathing VLEO imaging demonstration
summary: TelePIX and Bellatrix Aerospace are collaborating on a 2028 demonstration mission to test air-breathing propulsion for very low Earth orbit (VLEO) imaging. The project aims to advance geospatial capabilities using innovative satellite technology.

title: Spatial data has become a weapon of war in the US-Iran war
summary: Spatial data derived from satellites has become a critical weapon in the US-Iran conflict, marking a shift toward geospatial warfare. The distinction between civilian and military use of such data is increasingly blurred.

title: China’s latest batch of new and reusable rockets are close to launch
summary: China is preparing to launch several new and reusable rockets, including state-led and commercial models, with debuts and first-stage recovery attempts expected in the coming months.

title: New Glenn rocket explodes on Cape Canaveral pad
summary: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded during a hotfire test at Cape Canaveral on May 28, destroying the rocket and causing significant pad damage.

title: Space Force plans nationwide network of ‘resilient operations centers’
summary: The U.S. Space Force is planning to establish a nationwide network of resilient operations centers to ensure military space missions can continue during wartime. These facilities are designed to withstand potential attacks on installations.

title: Observable Space raises $90 million and wins Space Force contract for optical systems
summary: Observable Space raised $90 million and secured a U.S. Space Force contract to develop optical systems for laser communications and space domain awareness.

title: 'The Arrival' at 30: Charlie Sheen’s criminally underrated alien invasion thriller feels much scarier today
summary: The article reflects on the 30th anniversary of the movie 'The Arrival,' highlighting its themes of alien invasion and environmental neglect. It suggests the film feels more relevant today due to growing concerns about climate change and planetary stewardship.

title: Watch this bio-inspired Mars rover concept 'swim' through sand on curved wheels (video)
summary: A bio-inspired Mars rover concept uses curved wheels inspired by the sandfish lizard to navigate sand more effectively. The design aims to improve mobility for future Mars exploration missions.

title: Can solar sails really send humans out into interstellar space?
summary: Solar sails are being considered as a viable technology for human interstellar travel, with experts arguing they are not as futuristic as they seem. The concept is gaining serious attention for its potential in deep-space missions.

title: 'For All Mankind' star Wrenn Schmidt on her character's journey from NASA's mission control to the slammer (interview)
summary: The interview discusses Wrenn Schmidt's character in 'For All Mankind,' highlighting her journey from NASA's mission control to prison. The character's transformation includes exposure to new experiences and music like Dolly Parton.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 212 — Kabooms, Starship, and a Moon Base
summary: Episode 212 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast discusses the loss of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket, along with other topics like Starship and a Moon Base.

title: Watch the smallest full moon of 2026 tonight in this rare 'Blue Moon' livestream
summary: The smallest full moon of 2026 will be visible tonight via a livestream from Italy hosted by the Virtual Telescope Project. The event, called a 'Blue Moon,' begins at 9:30 p.m. EDT (0130 GMT).

title: From 'Tetris' to 'StarCraft 2' — 5 times astronauts took video games into space
summary: Astronauts have taken video games like Tetris and StarCraft 2 into space for entertainment. This highlights the recreational activities of astronauts during missions.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into Webb's 'Little Red Dots'. The discovery enhances understanding of early cosmic structures.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a major anomaly during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36, resulting in an explosion. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, utilizing rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative involves rebranding existing Commercial Lunar projects.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo satellite launches dominating the manifest. This highlights the growing commercial space activity in low Earth orbit.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station has been occupied with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance tasks throughout April. The activities highlight ongoing operations and research in low Earth orbit.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: China launched the Shenzhou 23 mission to send a new crew to the Tiangong space station, which has been continuously occupied since June 5, 2022.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, at 5:30:21 pm CDT from SpaceX's facility. The mission impressed observers with its performance during the inaugural flight.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's Starship Flight 12, Falcon 9, Rocket Lab's Electron, and Vega C missions.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website has published a news article related to the space industry, though the specific details are not provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space. Their work supports the success of ESA's missions by ensuring spacecraft components are space-proofed.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space-related activities.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' is vague and lacks context. It may refer to a spacecraft or satellite component designed for durability in harsh space environments.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry news article titled 'The great parachute bake-out' suggests a focus on parachute testing or technology for space missions. The image title implies a thermal or baking test process for parachutes.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling secure handling of objects in orbit. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn Explodes on the Pad
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a catastrophic failure during a launch attempt, resulting in an explosion on the pad. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's ambitions in the heavy-lift launch market.

title: Europe Celebrates its Sovereignty Progress at SmallSat
summary: Europe highlighted its progress in achieving orbital sovereignty at SmallSat Europe. The event emphasized the continent's advancements in space independence.

title: Observable Space Raises $90M Series A
summary: Observable Space, a space-tech startup, has raised $90 million in a Series A funding round to expand its production of laser and optical hardware and grow internationally beyond the US.

title: Space Markets Emerges From Stealth With Coinbase Ventures Investment
summary: Space Markets has emerged from stealth mode with a new investment from Coinbase Ventures. The company aims to create futures markets for space-related commodities and resources.

title: Exclusive: Stellar Alpina Raises $4.5M to Build Rotating Detonation Engines
summary: Stellar Alpina, a European propulsion startup, has raised $4.5 million to develop rotating detonation engines, a novel propulsion technology. This funding aims to advance a new kind of thrust mechanism for space applications.

title: Aitech Upgrades its Space Supercomputer
summary: Aitech has upgraded its space supercomputer by integrating NVIDIA's IGX Thor platform into the S-A2300 COTS AI Supercomputer, enhancing in-orbit data processing capabilities for customers.

title: NASA Awards LTV, Lunar Transport Contracts
summary: NASA has awarded contracts for Lunar Terrain Vehicle (LTV) and lunar transport services, marking a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon. The announcement was made during a press conference led by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman.

title: Op-ed: Commercial Demand in LEO is Lacking. The Government Can Help.
summary: A recent op-ed argues that the most promising market opportunity in Low Earth Orbit (LEO), in-space manufacturing, lacks sufficient commercial demand to sustain itself and requires government support to thrive.

title: Optical Links: When to Incorporate Them? When Not To?
summary: The article discusses the cost-effectiveness of optical links for satellite applications, noting that they often cost hundreds of thousands of dollars and may not be worth the investment for many use cases.

title: Satellogic Lands $18M+ Defense-Monitoring Contract
summary: Satellogic secured a defense-monitoring contract worth over $18 million, marking its first major agreement following a strategic pivot to persistent-monitoring capabilities.

title: SpaceX Gets Nearly $4.2 Billion Nod From Space Force For Initial SB-AMTI
summary: SpaceX received a nearly $4.2 billion contract from the U.S. Space Force for the initial phase of the SB-AMTI program. This follows a $2.3 billion award for the Space Data Network Backbone, a proliferated Low Earth Orbit communications system.

title: Sateliot Wins New Telenor IoT Deal
summary: Sateliot, a Spanish satellite company, has secured a new IoT partnership with Telenor IoT, a division of the Nordic telco giant Telenor Group.

title: Viasat Full Year ‘26 Takeaways: Record Revenue, Equatys Moves Forward
summary: Viasat reported record revenue and awards for the full year 2026, though it fell short of its projected stabilization in maritime and fixed broadband segments. The company also highlighted progress on its Equatys initiative.

title: Blue Origin’s Launch Pad Explosion Could Impact Timelines for Commercial Customers, NASA Missions
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a launch pad explosion at Cape Canaveral, potentially delaying commercial and NASA missions. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's launch timelines.

title: EU Targets User Terminal Tech Advancements Ahead of IRIS²
summary: The European Union has opened a new tender seeking proposals for cost-effective user terminal technologies for the IRIS² satellite constellation. This initiative aims to advance user terminal tech ahead of the constellation's deployment.

title: BlackSky Advances Non-Earth Imaging Business With 7-Figure Renewal
summary: BlackSky Technology secured a multi-million dollar contract renewal for its non-Earth imaging services, which involve capturing imagery of objects in orbit. This deal highlights the growing demand for space situational awareness and orbital debris monitoring.

title: UK’s O2 Satellite Expands D2D Service to iPhones
summary: UK’s O2 Satellite has expanded its direct-to-device (D2D) service to support iPhones, utilizing SpaceX’s Starlink Mobile constellation. The service, which initially launched earlier this year, now offers broader compatibility.

title: Swissto12 Adds to Leadership Team to Target Global Growth
summary: Swissto12 is expanding its leadership team with new appointments, including a chief commercial officer and executives focused on government affairs and partnerships, to support its global growth strategy.

title: Orbital Data Centers Must Tackle Chip Lifespan, Launch Availability, and Cybersecurity Challenges
summary: Orbital data centers face challenges like chip lifespan, launch availability, and cybersecurity. The topic gained attention following SpaceX's initial IPO documents.

title: Will the European Launch Market Finally Ignite?
summary: The European space industry is seeking more efficient and reliable access to space, a critical question discussed at a recent launch panel in Amsterdam. The focus is on when the European launch market will achieve significant growth and innovation.
